titel: Sweden's accession to the extended partial agreement for the Special Tribunal for the prosecution of acts of aggression against Ukraine.
html: Sweden has participated in the work to establish a tribunal with jurisdiction over Russia's acts of aggression against Ukraine. The tribunal was established in June 2024 through a bilateral agreement between Ukraine and the Council of Europe. For the tribunal to become operational, states wishing to become members must accede to an extended partial agreement regarding the tribunal's administrative committee, within the framework of the Council of Europe's institutional structure. The agreement regulates the tribunal's operation and entails an obligation for the acceding states to pay an annual fee through the Council of Europe. By acceding to the agreement, Sweden will become a member of the tribunal. This proposal suggests that the Swedish Parliament should approve Sweden's accession to the extended partial agreement.
tldr: Sweden is joining a special court that will prosecute those who initiated the war in Ukraine. With this decision, Sweden is helping to fund and operate the court through the Council of Europe.
lattlast:
Sweden has been involved in efforts to establish a special court. This court, called a tribunal, will have the authority to prosecute those responsible for initiating the Russian war in Ukraine. The court was created in June 2024 through an agreement between Ukraine and the Council of Europe, an organization with many member countries in Europe.

For the court to function, countries must sign an agreement outlining its governance. This agreement is called an extended partial agreement. By signing this agreement, Sweden will become a member of the court's governing body.

Membership also requires Sweden to pay an annual fee. These funds will be used to cover the court's operations and expenses. The government has now submitted a proposal to the Parliament for Sweden to join this effort.
